title: Warren Hamilton Defeats Larry Boggs, Margie Alfonso Defeats Clay Iiams
author: James Silberman
orientation: Free the States reports that abolitionist-endorsed candidates Warren Hamilton and Margie Alfonso won their 2020 Oklahoma Republican primary races against incumbent Senator Larry Boggs and Clay Iiams respectively, with Hamilton's upset particularly notable for overcoming a massive establishment money advantage. The article frames these wins as a significant breakthrough for the abolitionist movement against the pro-life Republican establishment.

questions.question: What were the actual margins of victory in the two primary races?
questions.answer: Warren Hamilton beat Larry Boggs 52.3 to 47.7 percent in the SD7 Senate primary runoff, while Margie Alfonso took 59.41 percent of the vote against Clay Iiams in the HD79 House race.
questions.quote: Warren Hamilton defeated incumbent State Senator Larry Boggs by a 52.3-to-47.7 margin in the SD7 primary runoff to become the second abolitionist to unseat a Republican establishment incumbent and the first to do so in Oklahoma.

questions.question: How significant was the financial disadvantage Hamilton overcame?
questions.answer: Boggs spent more than twice what Hamilton spent on his own campaign ($103,794.80 vs. $45,650.69), and on top of that two establishment PACs (Advance Oklahoma Fund and Senate Majority fund) reportedly poured in over a hundred thousand additional dollars supporting Boggs.
questions.quote: Boggs spent $103,794.80 to Hamilton's $45,650.69. Beyond the campaign expenditures, Boggs had two big-money establishment PACs (Advance Oklahoma Fund and Senate Majority fund) spending more than a hundred thousand dollars more on his behalf.

questions.question: What does Free the States want from Margie Alfonso if she is elected to the State House?
questions.answer: They want her to author The Abolition of Abortion in Oklahoma Act in the State House, which she has already promised to do as a condition of their qualified endorsement.
questions.quote: We gave Alfonso a qualified endorsement, and she has promised to author The Abolition of Abortion in Oklahoma Act in the State House should she be elected.

questions.question: Who else does Free the States have moving into the November 3 general election?
questions.answer: Ben Janloo, a qualified-endorsement candidate who didn't face a primary opponent, joins Hamilton and Alfonso in November, while Senator Roland Pederson was already reelected since he has no Democrat opponent.
questions.quote: Hamilton and Alfonso join Ben Janloo, who we also gave a qualified endorsement to, in the November 3 general election. Janloo did not face an opponent in the Republican primary.

questions.question: Why does Free the States consider Senator Pederson worth supporting?
questions.answer: Because Pederson actually voted to bring The Abolition of Abortion in Oklahoma Act out of committee during the 2020 legislative session, distinguishing him from establishment Republicans who blocked the bill.
questions.quote: Pederson voted to bring The Abolition of Abortion in Oklahoma Act out of committee during the 2020 legislative session.

questions.question: What general election opponents do the winning abolitionist candidates now face?
questions.answer: Hamilton will face Democrat Jerry Donathan in SD7, and Alfonso will face incumbent Democrat Melissa Provenzano in HD79, both on November 3.
questions.quote: Hamilton, who we endorsed, moves on to face Democrat Jerry Donathan in the general election on November 3.

questions.question: Why does the article single out Hamilton's win as more astonishing than Alfonso's?
questions.answer: Because Hamilton was outspent by Boggs's campaign and outside PACs by a wide margin and faced numerous prominent establishment endorsers, yet still won as an outspoken abolitionist.
questions.quote: Both victories are important, but Hamilton's victory is especially astonishing.

questions.question: How does Free the States characterize the political establishment Hamilton beat?
questions.answer: They describe it as a coalition of big-money PACs and prominent figures who joined forces behind Boggs, framing the loss as a defeat for entrenched political power rather than just one incumbent.
questions.quote: Numerous prominent establishment figures joined forces behind Boggs. With all of this money and these political connections on Boggs' side, Hamilton, the outspoken abolitionist, came out victorious.
